description:
Executor the desktop workflow application for Windows. Speed up your daily workflow, opening things and doing things.

Executor the desktop workflow application for Windows. Speed up your daily workflow, opening things and doing things.

Key Features:
- Launch applications, files, folders, websites, web services (with parameters) and prompts in milliseconds.
- Trigger multiple actions with one text command and/or hotkey.
- Let's be an expert in opening up the world instead of trying to put the whole world inside of Executor (as some alternatives try to do).
- Take the clipboard to a new level, swiftly trigger system commands, do fast calculations, manage running applications and more.
- Tailor keywords, appearance, hotkeys, and behavior to perfectly match your daily workflow.
- Customize the look and feel with various skins and theming options to match your desktop setup.
What's new
- Added that you can run a RegEx on the current clipboard content. This can be done by using the keyword command $TEXTREGEX$ and the keyword parameter should contain the RegEx. The parameter supports a slash convention s/pattern/replacement/ to transform clipboard text, or enter a pattern alone to automatically delete all matches. Here's an example keyword that uses a RegEx to remove the fixed text width (often 80 characters) copied from a terminal window: Keyword name=cleanterminaltext command=$TEXTREGEX$ parameter=s/\r?\n\s+(?=[a-z])/ / comment=Cleanup terminal text
- Added $TEXTREGEXPASTE$ does the same as $TEXTREGEX$ but also pastes the processed text (mostly useful for assigning to a hotkey).
- Added that when viewing clipboard by typing clipboard, and right clicking a clipboard item, all keywords that has a command that starts-with $TEXTREGEX (if any) will be listed in order to use it on that item.
- Added in the Expanded Edit View and right clicking, the menu will list all keywords that has a command that starts-with $TEXTREGEX (if any). If clicking one, that RegEx will be applied to all the current text in the Expanded Edit View.
Improvements
- The "Synthetic input support" setting now support more virtual input methods. The setting can be enabled in Settings > Input, typing & autocomplete.
